Sorcerer.scope: Endgame push (Pit / Torment) - Season 14, Lord of Hatred

entries.note: The season's real winner. Fire scaling got real help from Paragon and Heat changes, and the DoT ramp hits hard once it's rolling.
Still the smoothest top-tier Sorc experience even after the Unstable Currents nerf softened its ceiling.
Cold got quiet buffs this patch. Strong AoE clear with real crowd control on top.
Helm and Paragon buffs pushed this into real burst-damage territory this season.

excluded.name: Ball Lightning Sorcerer
excluded.reason: Icy Veins still lists this A-tier, but GAMES.GG (posted a day later) says plainly the old Ball Lightning meta is dead following the Unstable Currents rework. Direct source conflict on a build that's likely just outdated on one side - held back rather than guessed at.

excluded.name: Hydra Sorcerer
excluded.reason: Mentioned only by Leprestore, not corroborated elsewhere. Hydra guides have a specific history of going stale on us before (excluded from the original build library for the same reason) - needs a second independent source before it goes on a ranked list.

excluded.name: Charged Bolts Sorcerer / Fireball Sorcerer / Static Field Blizzard Sorcerer
excluded.reason: Single-source mentions (Icy Veins only), not cross-verified against the other two sources checked. Left off this pass.

Barbarian.scope: Endgame push (Pit / Torment) - Season 14, Lord of Hatred

entries.note: The safest recommendation in the class. Smooth, extremely tanky, and among the fastest farming builds in the game on top of a real Pit ceiling.

A Call of the Ancients scaling fix pulled this to the top of the leaderboard this patch - highest non-bugged DPS in the class, alternating Mighty Throw and Call of the Ancients as double ultimates. Worth noting: Icy Veins' own endgame table doesn't list an Ancients build by name at all, even though it's the newer, more detailed sources that call out this exact patch mechanic.
Season 14 item changes gave bleed-stacking Rend a real boost. Safer and more sustained than the S-tier picks, just slower to clear.
Still hits enormously hard and one-shots elites when fully optimized, but no longer the dominant force it was in earlier seasons - a notch below the S-tier picks.

Druid.scope: Endgame push (Pit / Torment) - Season 14, Lord of Hatred

entries.note: High mobility with a smooth, responsive rotation. Not the single highest raw damage in the class, but the overall playability is what earns the S tier.
A season of Storm-skill buffs pushed this well-established build back into the top tier.
Dolmen Stone buffs made this the season's refreshing pick, especially notable since most items got nerfed this patch.
The Storm Set overhaul gave pure Tornado a genuinely complete kit this season instead of a half-build.
Still playable, but the Shepherd's Aspect nerf (13% down to 3% damage per companion) gutted what used to be its top setup. A shadow of its former self, per both sources.

excluded.name: IGGM's Druid tier list (as a source)
excluded.reason: Published June 29, 2026 - one day before Season 14 actually launched. It's a pre-patch prediction, not verified post-launch data, even though it happens to land on the same S-tier picks (Shred, Lightning Storm) as the two live sources used here. Discounted as a citation on principle - agreement after the fact doesn't retroactively make a prediction a verified source.

Necromancer.scope: Endgame push (Pit / Torment) - Season 14, Lord of Hatred

entries.note: Still the top Necro build despite this patch's nerfs, though margins are tighter than last season.
The new home for Necro players after Banished Lord's Talisman got gutted. More relaxed, minion-focused playstyle.
Solid single-target and boss farming, benefiting from the new Mythic system's crit damage scaling.

excluded.name: Naz Mages / Minion Necromancer
excluded.reason: Icy Veins ranks a summoner build (Naz Mages / Mendeln Summoner) S-tier for endgame push, but GAMES.GG says generic Minion Necromancer 'falls off significantly in endgame Pit pushing.' Not clearly the same build under two names, and the two sources actively disagree on summoner necro's endgame viability - held back rather than picking a side.

excluded.name: Cold Sever Necromancer / Cold Army of the Dead / Blood Lance Necromancer
excluded.reason: Single-source mentions (Icy Veins only), not cross-verified, and none map cleanly onto an existing library build.

Rogue.scope: Endgame push (Pit / Torment) - Season 14, Lord of Hatred

entries.note: The safer top Rogue pick. Massive multi-hit scaling with far less risk than the bugged alternative.
The best leveling build in the game, and it transitions cleanly into endgame mapping.

excluded.name: Death Trap Rogue
excluded.reason: GAMES.GG confirms this is still the highest-ceiling build in the game right now (Pit 150) purely because of a bugged interaction. Same call as the original build library: not something we point players toward regardless of power level, given the hotfix risk.

excluded.name: Rapid Fire Rogue
excluded.reason: Single-source (Icy Veins only), and it cuts against our own library's read of that build as a low-complexity beginner pick rather than an endgame S-tier pusher. Held back pending a second source.

excluded.name: Twisting Blades Rogue
excluded.reason: GAMES.GG says it got direct damage buffs and is competitive again, but Icy Veins' endgame table doesn't list it at all. Single-sourced for current tier standing - not ranked this pass despite already being a Confirmed build in the library.

Spiritborn.scope: Endgame push (Pit / Torment) - Season 14, Lord of Hatred

entries.note: Still the top Spiritborn build and the best overall package after this patch's nerfs.
sources: Icy Veins (Jul 4, 2026, listed as Soar Swarm)

entries.note: Reliable ranged alternative, less affected by this patch's resolve-stacking nerfs, comfortable for all content.

excluded.name: Stinger Spiritborn
excluded.reason: Only GAMES.GG mentions it as a strong endgame-scaling pick. Icy Veins' endgame table doesn't include it. Single-sourced, held back.

excluded.name: Crushing Hand Spiritborn
excluded.reason: GAMES.GG calls it a budget/early-endgame option, not a top pick, and Icy Veins' endgame table omits it entirely. Neither source ranks it top-tier this season, consistent with our own library tagging it a beginner build.

excluded.name: Withering Fist Spiritborn
excluded.reason: Icy Veins-only mention with no corresponding build in our library to link it to.

Paladin.scope: Endgame push (Pit / Torment) - Season 14, Lord of Hatred

entries.note: GAMES.GG calls this outright the new top Paladin build this patch, following a Red Sermon damage buff. Icy Veins' own table places it a notch lower at A - noted here rather than picking one number and hiding the disagreement.

excluded.name: Blessed Shield Paladin
excluded.reason: Icy Veins-only S-tier mention, not corroborated by GAMES.GG. Held back pending a second source.

excluded.name: Wing Strikes Paladin
excluded.reason: GAMES.GG-only mention, not in Icy Veins' endgame table at all. Held back pending a second source.

excluded.name: Blessed Hammer builds (Hammerdin)
excluded.reason: Icy Veins-only mentions. Consistent with our own library tagging this a beginner-focused build rather than a top endgame pusher, so left off this ranked pass.
